governingCouncil.name: Pradyot Chandra Haldar
governingCouncil.about: Former Director, Intelligence Bureau (2008) and member of the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) 2011-2015.
governingCouncil.bio: Pradyot Chandra Haldar joined the Indian Police Service in 1970 and was allotted the Bihar cadre. During his distinguished career, he served across Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, Delhi, and the North-East region of India. He later joined the Intelligence Bureau (IB), where he served for over three decades before retiring in 2008 as Director, Intelligence Bureau. He is a recipient of the Police Medal for Meritorious Service and the President's Police Medal for Distinguished Service.Following his retirement, he served on several national-level committees dealing with critical issues of national security, including the Task Force for Security constituted by the Atomic Energy Commission, the Two-Member Committee reviewing the functioning of intelligence organisations, and the Union Government's Task Force on National Security. He was also a member of the National Security Advisory Board (NSAB) from 2011 to 2015 across two terms.Mr. Haldar has extensive experience in conflict resolution and peace negotiations, particularly in the North-East region of India. He played an active role in the peace process with Naga groups during the 1990s and contributed significantly towards bringing several militant organisations to the negotiating table over more than two decades. He continued to serve as the Government of India's Representative for Peace Talks until 31 December 2016. He currently serves as the President of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: Anil Prakash Bhatnagar
governingCouncil.about: IPS (Retd.), Former DGP, Punjab and Former Advisor to the Justice J. S. Verma Commission
governingCouncil.bio: Anil Prakash Bhatnagar, IPS (Punjab Cadre, 1968 Batch), is a distinguished former police officer with extensive experience in policing, human rights, prison reforms, and public administration. He holds a B.Sc. from Ewing Christian College, Allahabad University (1962), an LL.B. from Lucknow University (1964), and an M.A. from Muir Hostel, Allahabad University (1966). Prior to joining the Indian Police Service in 1968, he briefly served as a Probationary Officer with the State Bank of India.During his career, Mr. Bhatnagar served in several senior leadership positions, including Director General of Police (Prisons), Punjab, and Additional Director General of Police at the Punjab State Human Rights Commission. Following his retirement, he served as an Advisor to the GMR Group. He also served as Advisor to the Justice J. S. Verma Commission of Inquiry, contributing to critical discussions and recommendations on governance and justice-related issues. He has actively worked on initiatives related to child rights, child protection, and prison reforms, including collaborative projects with the British Council and the Foreign & Commonwealth Office, United Kingdom. He has also visited child protection centres in the UK as part of these engagements. Mr. Bhatnagar is the author of a report on prison reforms for the Government of Punjab and currently serves as the Secretary General of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: Dhirendra Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former Union Home Secretary and member of the Commission on Center State Relations.
governingCouncil.bio: Dhirendra Singh joined the Indian Administrative Service in 1968 and was in the Karnataka Cadre. He served in various capacities in the Karnataka State Government and in the Government of India and retired in 2005 after serving as the Union Home Secretary. Post retirement he was a member of the Commission on Center State Relations which rendered it's report in March 2010.

governingCouncil.name: Sanjay Sinha
governingCouncil.about: IPS (Retd.), Former Director General of Police, Tripura
governingCouncil.bio: Sanjay Sinha is a retired Indian Police Service (IPS) officer of the 1978 batch and former Director General of Police, Tripura. He holds an M.Sc. in Physics from Ranchi University and began his professional journey as a lecturer in Physics at Tata College, Chaibassa (Jharkhand). Prior to joining the IPS, he also briefly served as a Probationary Officer with the State Bank of Indore. He was allotted to the Manipur-Tripura cadre and subsequently served in Tripura. After his initial policing assignments in the state, Mr. Sinha joined the Intelligence Bureau on deputation, where he spent a major part of his distinguished professional career. During this period, he also served at the Embassy of India in Washington, D.C. Following his deputation, he returned to Tripura and later headed the state police force as Director General of Police, Tripura.During his tenure as DGP, Tripura Police was awarded the prestigious President’s Colour in January 2012 by the Vice President of India, becoming only the fourth State Police force in the country to receive the honour for its exceptional success in combating insurgency. Mr. Sinha is an alumnus of the National Defence College and a recipient of both the Indian Police Medal (IPM) and the President’s Police Medal (PPM). He currently serves as Treasurer of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: K M Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former Director General, Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) and Former Member, National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA)
governingCouncil.bio: K. M. Singh is a former Indian Police Service (IPS) officer with extensive experience in intelligence, internal security, and disaster management. He served in the Intelligence Bureau (Government of India) for nearly three decades and held several key assignments during his distinguished career. Between 1996 and 2000, he served as Minister (Coordination) at the Indian High Commission in London, and later as Director General of the Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) from 2003 to 2005. During his tenure with the Intelligence Bureau, he also served in Jammu & Kashmir for seven years. Following his retirement from service, Mr. Singh was appointed as a Member of the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) in 2005 with the status of Minister of State in the Union Government. During his eight-year tenure at NDMA, he played a pioneering role in establishing and developing the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) from its inception into one of the country’s premier disaster response forces. He was also a member of the Steering Committee constituted in 2003 to formulate the national response mechanism for disaster management at the national level. In recognition of his outstanding contribution to disaster management and institution-building, Mr. Singh was awarded the prestigious Subhash Chandra Bose Aapda Prabandhan Puraskar by the Government of India in 2020. He is presently associated with the Policy Perspectives Foundation, where he has taken pioneering initiatives in the field of management of animals during emergencies and disasters.

governingCouncil.name: Gen ( Retd. ) N C Vij
governingCouncil.about: Former Chief of Army Staff and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: General (Retd.) N. C. Vij served as the 21st Chief of the Army Staff of the Indian Army from December 2002 to January 2005. During his distinguished military career, he played a key role as Director General of Military Operations (DGMO) during the 1999 Kargil conflict, contributing significantly to the successful operations undertaken by the Indian Armed Forces. Following his retirement from the Army, General Vij was appointed the Founder Vice Chairman of the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) with the rank of Cabinet Minister, serving from 2005 to 2010. He later served as Director of the Vivekananda International Foundation from 2014 to 2017. He is currently associated with the Policy Perspectives Foundation as a Member of the Governing Council.

governingCouncil.name: Sankar Sen
governingCouncil.about: Former Director General, NHRC and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Sankar Sen is a retired IPS officer of the 1960 batch from the Odisha cadre and has held several distinguished positions in policing, human rights, and public administration. He served as Director General at the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) and also as Director of the National Police Academy, Hyderabad. A Rhodes Scholar, Shri Sen has consistently contributed to policy discussions and institutional reforms in areas relating to policing, prisons, governance, and human rights. He is presently a Senior Fellow at the Institute of Social Sciences and regularly contributes articles to leading newspapers including The Statesman, The Tribune, and The Pioneer. Shri Sen has authored several books on human rights and socially relevant issues and is frequently invited to seminars, police academies, administrative institutions, and correctional facilities to provide intellectual and reform-oriented perspectives. He currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: T C A Rangachari
governingCouncil.about: Former Ambassador and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Ambassador (Retd.) T. C. A. Rangachari is a distinguished former Indian Foreign Service officer with over 36 years of diplomatic experience across Asia, Europe, and multilateral institutions. He served as India’s Ambassador to Algeria, France, and Germany, and also spent over five years in Islamabad as Deputy High Commissioner to Pakistan. His diplomatic assignments included postings in Hong Kong, Beijing during the Cultural Revolution, and India’s Permanent Mission to the United Nations in New York.Over the course of his career, Ambassador Rangachari worked extensively on issues relating to international relations, human rights, development cooperation, and public diplomacy. He represented India on the governing boards of UNDP, UNFPA, and UNICEF, and also served in various divisions of the Ministries of External Affairs and Finance in New Delhi, including as Chief Passport Officer of the Government of India. He holds an M.A. in History from Delhi University and currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: Shekhar Sinha
governingCouncil.about: Vice Admiral (Retd.) and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Vice Admiral Shekhar Sinha, PVSM, AVSM, NM & Bar, is a distinguished former officer of the Indian Navy and one of India’s senior-most naval aviators. Commissioned into the Executive Branch of the Indian Navy in 1974 as a fighter pilot, he has held several prestigious operational and leadership appointments, including Chief of Integrated Defence Staff (IDS), Flag Officer Commanding Western Fleet, Assistant Chief of Naval Staff (Air), and Flag Officer Naval Aviation. With over 2,700 flying hours, primarily on aircraft carriers INS Vikrant and INS Viraat flying Sea Harrier aircraft, Vice Admiral Sinha has commanded naval air squadrons, major warships, and the Western Fleet. He has also played important operational roles during Operation Pawan and Operation Parakram. An alumnus of the Defence Services Staff College, College of Naval Warfare, and National Defence College, he remains actively engaged in strategic and defence affairs and currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: Prof. Sankarshan Basu
governingCouncil.about: Professor, IIM Bangalore and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Professor Sankarshan Basu is a distinguished academic and finance expert associated with the Indian Institute of Management Bangalore, where he teaches in the areas of Finance and Accounting. He has previously served as Chairperson of the Post Graduate Programme in Public Policy and Management, Career Development Services, and Alumni Relations at IIM Bangalore. His academic background includes degrees from Presidency College Kolkata, IIT Kanpur, and a Ph.D. from the London School of Economics and Political Science, UK. His research and teaching interests focus on financial markets, derivatives, insurance, pensions, and quantitative finance. Prior to joining academia, he worked with ICICI Ltd. and has also taught at institutions including the London School of Economics and Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh. Professor Basu has held visiting appointments at universities across Europe and Asia and has published extensively in the fields of finance and public policy. He currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: P K Malhotra
governingCouncil.about: Former Union Law Secretary and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Mr. P. K. Malhotra is a distinguished legal expert with over four decades of experience in legislative drafting, public policy, judicial administration, and governance. As an officer of the Indian Legal Service, he served in the Ministry of Law & Justice for more than 25 years and eventually rose to the position of Union Law Secretary. During his tenure, he played a significant role in the formulation and processing of several landmark legislations and constitutional amendments. His contributions include work on the Companies Act, 2013, Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, GST Constitutional Amendment, Commercial Courts Act, Consumer Protection reforms, and various electoral and judicial reforms. Mr. Malhotra has also served as Member of the Law Commission of India, Judicial Member of the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal, Presiding Officer of the Securities Appellate Tribunal, and Chairman of the Adjudicating Authority under the Prevention of Money Laundering framework. He currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

governingCouncil.name: R P Singh
governingCouncil.about: Former CMD, Power Grid Corporation of India and Member, Governing Council, PPF
governingCouncil.bio: Dr. R. P. Singh is a distinguished corporate leader and former Chairman and Managing Director of Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL). With over 37 years of professional experience, he has worked extensively in engineering, contract management, infrastructure development, and human resource management. Prior to his leadership at PGCIL, he was also associated with NTPC and Tata Steel. During his tenure, PGCIL emerged as one of India’s leading power transmission companies and was successfully listed on the stock exchanges in 2007. Dr. Singh has contributed significantly to India’s infrastructure and energy sector development and continues to engage with institutional and policy initiatives. He currently serves as a Member of the Governing Council of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

distinguishedFellows.name: Prof. Gautam Sen
distinguishedFellows.about: Professor Emeritus and Distinguished Fellow, PPF
distinguishedFellows.bio: Professor Gautam Sen is a noted scholar and expert in strategic affairs, defence studies, and national security. Over several decades, he has contributed extensively to academic research, policy discussions, and strategic thought in India. He has authored multiple books and papers on national security, defence strategy, and India’s strategic outlook, including works such as National Interest and National Security Policymaking Prism for India and The Purpose of India’s Security Strategy. Professor Sen has held several distinguished academic and institutional positions, including Savarkar Professor and Head of the Department of Defence & Strategic Studies at the University of Pune. He has also served as Director of the National Centre of International Security & Defence Analysis and has been associated with leading institutions including CLAWS, NIAS Bengaluru, and various strategic studies centres in India. He continues to contribute actively to research, defence education, and policy dialogue as a Distinguished Fellow of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

distinguishedFellows.name: Anwar Alam
distinguishedFellows.about: Distinguished Fellow focusing on International Relations, Global Governance, and Muslim Societies.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Professor Anwar Alam is an eminent scholar of international relations, West Asian politics, political theory, and global governance. He has held academic positions at several leading institutions including Jamia Millia Islamia, Jawaharlal Nehru University, Aligarh Muslim University, and Zirve University, Turkey. His academic work spans issues relating to political Islam, minority politics, global governance, and Middle Eastern affairs. Over the years, Professor Alam has received numerous prestigious fellowships, including the Alexander von Humboldt Fellowship (Germany), ICSSR Doctoral Fellowship, Indo-French Social Scientist Exchange Fellowship, and the International Visitor Programme Scholarship in the United States. He has authored and edited several important publications on international politics, Muslim societies, radicalisation, and India’s engagement with West Asia. He currently serves as a Distinguished Fellow of the Policy Perspectives Foundation.

distinguishedFellows.name: Anil Kumar Das
distinguishedFellows.about: Development practitioner with extensive experience in strategic planning, research, and monitoring evaluation.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Anil Kumar Das is a development practitioner and policy consultant with extensive experience in strategic planning, research, monitoring and evaluation, documentation, advocacy, project management, and partnership building. Since 1999, he has worked across diverse development sectors, contributing to both grassroots interventions and policy-oriented initiatives through research-driven and implementation-focused approaches. Over the course of his career, he has undertaken qualitative and quantitative research, developed theoretical and operational frameworks, prepared training manuals, managed action-oriented projects, and documented development interventions and institutional processes. He has authored more than 60 research studies, monitoring and evaluation reports, academic papers, and publications. In addition to his association with the Policy Perspectives Foundation, he currently serves as Director of the Human Development Society and is associated with organisations including UNICEF India and United Nations Development Programme as a policy and social development consultant. He holds postgraduate, M.Phil., and Ph.D. degrees from University of Delhi and Jawaharlal Nehru University.

distinguishedFellows.name: Manika Malhotra Jain
distinguishedFellows.about: Economics and policy analyst specializing in environment, child rights, and national security FDI perspectives.
distinguishedFellows.bio: Manika Malhotra Jain is an economics and policy research professional with over nine years of experience in research, policy analysis, project coordination, and development-oriented initiatives. She holds a postgraduate degree in Economics from TERI School of Advanced Studies and a Bachelor's degree in Economics from Delhi University. Alongside her professional engagements, she is currently pursuing doctoral research in the field of Business Economics and Management. Her work combines qualitative and quantitative research methodologies with a strong focus on governance, sustainability, social development, and policy implementation. Over the years, she has successfully led and coordinated multiple research, policy, and social development projects across sectors including child rights, women empowerment, sustainability, public health, governance, and inclusive development. She has been a lead researcher on several significant studies, including a comprehensive analysis of the implementation of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act, 2012. She also authored a monograph on E-Waste Management: Policy Analysis and Implementation, focusing on reverse logistics, circular economy frameworks, and policy implementation in the Delhi-NCR region. Manika has co-edited books and written extensively on sustainability, women's safety, informal labour, FDI, and public policy. Her articles and policy commentaries have appeared in publications including The Hindu, The Indian Express, Firstpost, CNBC, and Business Standard. Her professional experience also includes consulting assignments with the Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG), Ministry of Home Affairs, in collaboration with the Vision India Foundation, along with engagements across academic and private sector institutions.
distinguishedFellows.subjectsOfInterest: Economics, Environment, Child Rights, Public Policy, and Social Development
